abstract:
Abstract
                  In this digital age, the adoption of digital technology (DT) is changing traditional business processes. Against this backdrop, we try to answer the question of whether DT could effectively bridge any interfirm knowledge capability gaps. Though effective knowledge transfer is dependent on the sender's and the receiver's knowledge capabilities, literature attributes very limited attention to their combined effects. Using signalling metaphor as the guiding framework, we forward disseminative capacity and absorptive capacity as the key capabilities of the sender and receiver, respectively, and study their combined effects on relational performance within vertical interfirm relationships. Additionally, with the belief that comprehending how DT could interplay with these capabilities holds crucial importance, we test the conjecture that DT can bridge knowledge capability gaps pertaining to the sender or the receiver. We position our hypotheses using the relational view and test them using the response surface methodology. The analysis is conducted based on a multi‐respondent dyadic survey dataset that is augmented with secondary data and longitudinal performance data. Our findings showcase that disseminative capacity and absorptive capacity are natural counterparts and that the channel effectiveness resulting from adopting DT could serve as an offsetting capability that can effectively bridge certain knowledge capability gaps.

affiliation: The University of Texas at Dallas
authors_full.name: Vaidyanathan Jayaraman

ob.ai_reason: 数字技术在知识转移中的作用，涉及技术但非特指AI
ob.tldr: 本研究基于信号理论与关系视角，采用双层调查与响应曲面法检验数字技术是否能补偿企业间的知识能力差距。结果表明，传播能力与吸收能力相互补足，数字技术通过提升渠道效能可有效弥补发送方或接收方的能力缺口，提升跨组织知识转移绩效。

title: Bridging Knowledge Capability Gaps: Digital Technology Offsetting in Interfirm Knowledge Transfer
title_zh: 弥合知识能力差距：企业间知识转移中的数字技术补偿效应
